title: Equivalent expressions with percent problems
description: Create expressions with fractions and decimals to solve real world percent word problems.

assessment_items.question_content:
In March, Delphine's house had $40\%$ more snowfall than in February.  Delphine's house had $f$ centimeters of snowfall in February.

**Which of the following expressions could represent how much snowfall Delphine had at her house in March?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
Delphine's house had $\maroonE{f}$ centimeters of snowfall in February, and there were $40\%$ more centimeters of snowfall in March. 

So there were $\goldE{40\%\cdot f}$ *more* centimeters of snowfall in March than in February.
That means her house had a total of $\goldE{40\% \cdot f} + \maroonE{f}$ centimeters of snowfall in March.

Because $40\%$ means $40$ *per hundred*, we can write it in equivalent forms.

$\begin{align}
40\%&=\dfrac{40}{100}\\\\
&=\dfrac{4}{10}\\\\
&=0.4
\end{align}$

Let's find equivalent ways of writing the amount of snowfall Delphine's house had in March.

$\begin{align}
\blueE{40\%} \cdot f + f&=\blueE{\dfrac{4}{10}}f+f\\\\
&= \blueE{0.4}f+f\\\\
&=\blueE{0.4}f+1f\\\\
&=(\blueE{0.4}+1)f\\\\
&=1.4f
\end{align}$
The expressions $\dfrac{4}{10}f+f$ and $1.4f$ each could represent how much snowfall Delphine had at her house in March.

assessment_items.question_content:
Eiko is wearing a magic ring that increases the power of her healing spell by $30\%$. Without the ring, her healing spell restores $H$ health points.

**Which of the following expressions could represent how many health points the spell restores when Eiko is wearing the magic ring?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
The healing spell restores $\maroonD{H}$ health points normally, and with Eiko's ring, the spell restores $30\%$ more health points.

The healing spell restores $\greenD{30\%\cdot H}$ *more* health points with the ring than without it.
That means the healing spell restores $\greenD{30\%\cdot H}+\maroonD{H}$ health points with the ring.

Because $30\%$ means $30$ *per hundred*, we can write it in equivalent forms.

$\begin{align}
30\%&=\dfrac{30}{100}\\\\
&=\dfrac{3}{10}\\\\
&=0.3
\end{align}$

Let's find equivalent ways of writing the number of health points the spell restores when Eiko wears her ring.

$\begin{align}
\blueD{30\%}\cdot H+H&=\blueD{\dfrac{3}{10}}H+H\\\\
&=\left(\blueD{\dfrac{3}{10}}+1\right)H\\\\
&=\left(\blueD{\dfrac{30}{100}}+1\right)H\\\\
&=\dfrac{130}{100}H
\end{align}$
The expressions $\left({\dfrac{3}{10}}+1\right)H$ and $\dfrac{130}{100}H$ each could represent how many health points the spell restores when Eiko is wearing the magic ring.

assessment_items.question_content:
Crispy Clover, a popular vegetarian restaurant, introduced a new menu that  has $20\%$ more dishes than the previous menu. The previous menu had $D$ dishes.

**Which of the following expressions could represent how many dishes Crispy Clover's new menu has?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
The previous menu had $\maroonD{D}$ dishes, and there are $20\%$ more dishes on the new menu. 

So there were $\greenD{20\%\cdot D}$ *more* dishes on the new menu compared to the old menu.
That means there are a total of $\greenD{20\% \cdot D} + \maroonD{D}$ dishes on the new menu.

Because $20\%$ means $20$ *per hundred*, we can write it in equivalent forms.

$\begin{align}
20\%&=\dfrac{20}{100}\\\\
&=\dfrac{1}{5}\\\\
&=0.2
\end{align}$

Let's find equivalent ways of writing the number of dishes on the new menu.

$\begin{align}
\blueD{20\%} \cdot D + D&=\blueD{\dfrac{1}{5}}D+D\\\\
&= \blueD{0.2}D+D\\\\
&=\blueD{0.2}D+1D\\\\
&=(\blueD{0.2}+1)D\\\\
&=1.2D
\end{align}$
The expressions $D+\dfrac{1}{5}D$ and $1.2D$ each could represent the number of dishes on the new menu.

assessment_items.question_content:
After the outbreak of a mysterious disease, the average wait time at a hospital increased by $70\%$. Before the outbreak, the average wait time at the hospital was $m$ minutes.

**Which of the following expressions could represent the average wait time at the hospital after the outbreak?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
The average wait time was $\maroonD{m}$ minutes before the outbreak, and the wait time increased by $70\%$ after the outbreak.

So the average wait time is $\greenD{70\%\cdot m}$ *longer* after the outbreak.
That means the average wait time after the outbreak is $\greenD{70\% \cdot m} + \maroonD{m}$ minutes.

Because $70\%$ means $70$ *per hundred*, we can write it in equivalent forms.

$\begin{align}
70\%&=\dfrac{70}{100}\\\\
&=\dfrac{7}{10}\\\\
&=0.7
\end{align}$

Let's find equivalent ways of writing the average wait time after the outbreak.

$\begin{align}
\blueD{70\%} \cdot m + m&=\blueD{\dfrac{7}{10}}m+m\\\\
&= \blueD{0.7}m+m\\\\
&=\blueD{0.7}m+1m\\\\
&=(\blueD{0.7}+1)m\\\\
&=1.7m \\\\
&=\dfrac{170}{100}m
\end{align}$
The expressions $1.7m$ and $\dfrac{170}{100}m$ each could represent the average wait time, in minutes, after the outbreak.

assessment_items.question_content:
Last Sunday, the average temperature was $8\%$ higher than the average temperature two Sundays ago. The average temperature two Sundays ago was $T$ degrees Celsius.

**Which of the following expressions could represent the average temperature last Sunday?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
The average temperature two Sundays ago was  $\maroonD{T}$ degrees Celsius, and it was $8\%$ higher last Sunday.

So the average temperature was $\greenD{8\%\cdot T}$ *higher* last Sunday than it was two Sundays ago.
That means the temperature last Sunday was $\greenD{8\% \cdot T} + \maroonD{T}$ degrees Celsius.

Because $8\%$ means $8$ *per hundred*, we can write it in equivalent forms.

$\begin{align}
8\%&=\dfrac{8}{100}\\\\
&=0.08
\end{align}$

Let's find equivalent ways of writing the average temperature last Sunday.

$\begin{align}
\blueD{8\%} \cdot T + T&=\blueD{\dfrac{8}{100}}T+T\\\\
&=\left(\dfrac{8}{100}+1\right)T\\\\
&=\left(1+\dfrac{8}{100}\right)T\\\\
&=\dfrac{108}{100}T\\\\
&=1.08T
\end{align}$
The expressions $1.08T$ and $\left(1+\dfrac{8}{100}\right)T$ each could represent the average temperature last Sunday.

assessment_items.question_content:
Bo is buying a board game that  usually costs $B$ dollars. The game is on sale, and the price has been reduced by $18\%$.

**Which of the following expressions could represent how much Bo pays for the game?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
The discount is $18\%$ of the price $B$ of the board game.

So the board game has a discount of $\greenD{18\%\cdot B}$ dollars.
That means that Bo pays $\maroonD{B}-\greenD{18\%\cdot B}$ dollars for the game.

Because $18\%$ means $18$ *per hundred*, we can write it in equivalent forms.

$\begin{align}
18\%&=\dfrac{18}{100}\\\\
&=\dfrac{9}{50}\\\\
&=0.18
\end{align}$

Let's find equivalent ways of writing the amount Bo pays for the board game.

$\begin{align}
B-\blueD{18\%}\cdot B&=B-\blueD{\dfrac{18}{100}}B\\\\
&=B-\blueD{0.18}B\\\\
&=B(1-0.18)\\\\
&=B(0.82)\\\\
&=0.82B
\end{align}$
The expressions $B-{0.18}B$ and $0.82B$ each could represent how much Bo pays for the game.

assessment_items.question_content:
Landen spent $L$ hours at the beach last weekend. Matéo spent $15\%$ fewer hours at the beach than Landen did.

**Which of the following expressions could represent how many hours Matéo spent at the beach last weekend?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
Landen spent $\maroonD{L}$ hours at the beach, and Matéo spent $15\%$ fewer hours than Landen did.

So Matéo spent $\greenD{15\%\cdot L}$ *fewer* hours at the beach than Landen did.
That means Matéo spent $\maroonD{L}-\greenD{15\% \cdot L}$ hours at the beach last weekend.

Because $15\%$ means $15$ *per hundred*, we can write it in equivalent forms.

$\begin{align}
15\%&=\dfrac{15}{100}\\\\
&=\dfrac{3}{20}\\\\
&=0.15
\end{align}$

Let's find equivalent ways of writing the number of hours Matéo spent at the beach last weekend.

$\begin{align}
L-\blueD{15\%} \cdot L&=L-\blueD{\dfrac{3}{20}}L\\\\
&=L-\blueD{0.15}L\\\\
&=L(1-\blueD{0.15})\\\\
&=L(0.85)
\end{align}$
The expressions $L(1-{0.15})$ and $L-{\dfrac{3}{20}}L$ each could represent how many hours Matéo spent at the beach last weekend.

assessment_items.question_content:
A new soup recipe contains $33\%$ less sodium per serving than the old soup recipe. The old soup recipe contained $x$ milligrams of sodium per serving.

**Which of the following expressions could represent the amount of sodium per serving, in milligrams, in the new soup recipe?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
The old soup recipe contains $\maroonD{x}$ milligrams of sodium per serving. The new recipe contains $\greenD{33\%}$ less sodium per serving than the old recipe.

This means each serving of the new soup recipe contains $\greenD{33\%\cdot x}$ milligrams *less* sodium than each serving of the old soup recipe.
That means each serving of the new soup recipe contains $\maroonD{x}-\greenD{33\% \cdot x}$ milligrams of sodium.

Because $33\%$ means $33$ *per hundred*, we can write it in equivalent forms.

$\begin{align}
33\%&=\dfrac{33}{100}\\\\
&=0.33
\end{align}$

Let's find equivalent ways of writing the amount of sodium in each serving of the new soup recipe. 

$\begin{align}
x-\blueD{33\%} \cdot x&=x-\blueD{\dfrac{33}{100}}x\\\\
&=x\left(1-\blueD{\dfrac{33}{100}}\right)\\\\
&=x(1-\blueD{0.33})\\\\
&=x(0.67)\\\\
&=0.67x 
\end{align}$
The expressions $0.67x$ and $\left(1-\dfrac{33}{100}\right)x$ each could represent the amount of sodium per serving, in milligrams, in the new soup recipe.

assessment_items.question_content:
After Batman brought the Joker to justice, the crime rate in Arkham City decreased by $12\%$. Previously, the crime rate was $c$ incidents per $1000$ people.

**Which of the following expressions could represent the crime rate in Arkham City after Batman brought the Joker to justice?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
The crime rate in Arkham City was $\maroonD{c}$ incidents per $1000$ people. Batman reduced the crime rate by $\greenD{12\%}$ by bringing the Joker to justice.

So the crime rate was $\greenD{12\%\cdot c}$ incidents per $1000$ people *lower*.
That means after Batman brought the Joker to justice, the crime rate in Arkham City was $\maroonD{c}-\greenD{12\% \cdot c}$ incidents per $1000$ people.

Because $12\%$ means $12$ *per hundred*, we can write it in equivalent forms.

$\begin{align}
12\%&=\dfrac{12}{100}\\\\
&=\dfrac{3}{25}\\\\
&=0.12
\end{align}$

Let's find equivalent ways of writing the crime rate in Arkham City after Batman brought the Joker to justice.

$\begin{align}
c-\blueD{12\%} \cdot c&=c-\blueD{0.12}c\\\\
&=c(1-\blueD{0.12})\\\\
&=c(0.88)\\\\
&=0.88c \\\\
&=\dfrac{88}{100}c\\\\
&=\dfrac{22}{25}c
\end{align}$
The expressions $0.88c$ and $\dfrac{22}{25}c$ each could represent the crime rate in Arkham City after Batman brought the Joker to justice.

assessment_items.question_content:
After learning how to fly, Wendy reduced her daily commute time by $75\%$. Previously, her commute took $m$ minutes.

**Which of the following expressions could represent Wendy's commute time, in minutes, after she learned how to fly?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
Before learning how to fly, Wendy spent $\maroonD{m}$ minutes on her commute daily. After learning how to fly, she spends $75\%$ less time commuting.

So Wendy spends $\greenD{75\%\cdot m}$ *fewer* minutes commuting daily than she used to.
That means Wendy spends $\maroonD{m}-\greenD{75\% \cdot m}$ minutes commuting.

Because $75\%$ means $75$ *per hundred*, we can write it in equivalent forms.

$\begin{align}
75\%&=\dfrac{75}{100}\\\\
&=\dfrac{3}{4}\\\\
&=0.75
\end{align}$

Let's find equivalent ways of writing Wendy's commute time after she learned how to fly.

$\begin{align}
m-\blueD{75\%} \cdot m&=m-\blueD{0.75}m\\\\
&=m(1-\blueD{0.75})\\\\
&=m(0.25)\\\\
&=0.25m \\\\
&=\dfrac{1}{4}m
\end{align}$
The expressions $0.25m$ and $\dfrac{1}{4}m$ each could represent Wendy's commute time, in minutes, after she learned how to fly.

assessment_items.question_content:
On Monday, Florencia's hair was $h$ centimeters long. She got a haircut on Tuesday, so her hair was only $75\%$ of the length it was on Monday. 

**Which of the following expressions could represent how many centimeters long Florencia's hair was after the haircut?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
Florencia's hair was $\maroonD{h}$ centimeters long before the haircut, and after the haircut, her hair was $75\%$ as long.

So Florencia's hair was $\greenD{75\% \cdot h}$ centimeters long after the haircut.

Because $75\%$ means $75$ *per hundred*, we can write it in equivalent forms.

$\begin{align}
75\%&=\dfrac{75}{100}\\\\
&=\dfrac{3}{4}\\\\
&=0.75
\end{align}$

Let's find equivalent ways of writing the length of Florencia's hair after her haircut.

$\begin{align}
\blueD{75\%} \cdot h&=\blueD{0.75} h\\\\
&=\blueD{\dfrac{3}{4}} h
\end{align}$
The expressions ${0.75} h$ and ${\dfrac{3}{4}} h$ each could represent how many centimeters long Florencia's hair was after the haircut.

assessment_items.question_content:
A caterpillar eats $1400\%$ of its birth mass in one day. The caterpillar's birth mass is $m$ grams.

**Which of the following expressions could represent the amount, in grams, the caterpillar eats in one day?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
The caterpillar's birth mass is $\maroonD{m}$ grams. It eats $1400\%$ of its birth mass in one day.

So the caterpillar eats $\greenD{1400\% \cdot m}$ grams of food in one day.

Because $1400\%$ means $1400$ *per hundred*, we can write it in equivalent forms.

$\begin{align}
1400\%&=\dfrac{1400}{100}\\\\
&=14
\end{align}$

Let's find equivalent ways of writing the amount the caterpillar eats.

$\begin{align}
\blueD{1400\%} \cdot m&=\blueD{14} m\\\\
&=\blueD{\dfrac{1400}{100}} m
\end{align}$
The expressions $14m$ and $\dfrac{1400}{100}m$ each could represent the amount of food, in grams, the caterpillar eats in one day.
